params.name: Hina Doll
params.description:
On Nov 27, 1924 AD, the earliest Hina Doll was created in Japan.
The Hina Doll is a puppet made based on ancient Japanese court figures and is regarded as a magic tool. It is often displayed on a stepped wooden altar covered with red cloth along with various decorations. The doll is known for its exquisite features. People will spruce the doll up a day before the Girl's Day to ward off disasters for the girls in the family, and put it away again after the festival.
